name: Kay Kavus
description: Kay Kavus, a legendary king of Iran from the Kayanian dynasty, is a complex figure in Persian mythology, known for both his grandeur and his hubris. He is a prominent character in Ferdowsi's Shahnameh (Book of Kings), where he reigns for 150 years. Kavus is portrayed as a powerful and ambitious ruler, possessing immense wealth and a vast army. He is celebrated for expanding his kingdom and bringing prosperity to his people. However, his insatiable desire for power and his arrogance repeatedly lead him into perilous situations. He famously attempts to fly to heaven on a throne carried by eagles, a testament to his overweening pride and disregard for divine boundaries. This act of hubris results in his downfall, as he is punished by the gods. Despite his flaws, Kay Kavus remains a significant figure, embodying the dangers of unchecked ambition and the importance of humility. His stories serve as cautionary tales, highlighting the consequences of defying fate and divine order. He is ultimately rescued by the hero Rostam, solidifying Rostam's position as a central figure in Persian lore. Kavus' reign, filled with both triumphs and disasters, contributes significantly to the rich tapestry of Persian mythology, offering valuable lessons about leadership, morality, and the human condition. His story is a reminder that even the most powerful rulers are subject to human frailties and the whims of destiny.
subtitle: Ambitious King, Hubris, and Downfall
achievements: Inspired tales of heroism and caution

keyMyths.title: Attempt to Fly to Heaven
keyMyths.description: Kay Kavus, consumed by pride, attempts to fly to heaven on a throne carried by eagles. This audacious act angers the gods and leads to his punishment, showcasing the dangers of hubris.

keyMyths.title: The Invasion of Mazandaran
keyMyths.description: Kay Kavus leads an ill-fated invasion of Mazandaran, a land inhabited by powerful demons. He is captured, and Rostam is tasked with rescuing him from this perilous situation.

keyMyths.title: The Hamavaran War
keyMyths.description: Kay Kavus wages war against the Hamavaran King, leading to further conflicts and challenges. This war highlights Kavus's tendency to engage in unnecessary and often disastrous military campaigns.

sources.author: Ferdowsi
sources.description: The primary source for the legends and stories of Kay Kavus.
sources.text: The primary source for the legends and stories of Kay Kavus.
